text: Decide on Node embedding quality vs dependency tradeoff
description:
**HUMAN DECISION REQUIRED**

Current local architecture uses deterministic in-process Node embedding generator (hashed, fast, no dependencies). 

**Decision needed:** Should future local releases adopt higher-quality model-backed embeddings?

**Current approach: Hash-based local embeddings**
- Pro: Zero external dependencies (no Python)
- Pro: Fast, deterministic
- Pro: Works offline
- Con: Lower semantic search quality
- Con: Less intelligent aim similarity

**Option A: Upgrade to Node-native transformer model**
- Use @xenova/transformers (ONNX Runtime in Node)
- Pro: Better semantic search quality
- Pro: Still Node-only (no Python)
- Con: ~100-200MB model download on first run
- Con: Slower embedding generation
- Con: Requires ~1GB RAM for model

**Option B: Keep hash-based for default, add optional model path**
- Default: Fast hashed embeddings
- Advanced users: Can configure model path
- Pro: Best of both worlds
- Con: More configuration complexity

**Option C: Keep current approach**
- Accept lower quality for simplicity
- Pro: Simplest, most reliable
- Con: Search quality ceiling

**After deciding:** Update embedding configuration, document in README, implement if upgrading.
